events.title: Israeli Airstrikes Intensify in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Israel has intensified its airstrikes across southern Lebanon, with the health ministry reporting 22 fatalities, including eight children. Multiple raids targeted areas south of Beirut and other locations in the country's south and east. These strikes occurred as new US-brokered peace talks between Lebanon and Israel were set to begin in Washington, despite an ongoing ceasefire.

events.title: Saudi and Kuwaiti Strikes Against Iran-Backed Militias in Iraq
events.desc: Sources indicate that Saudi fighter jets conducted strikes against targets linked to powerful Tehran-backed Shi'ite militias in Iraq during the ongoing conflict with Iran. Additionally, retaliatory strikes were launched from Kuwait into Iraq. These actions are part of a broader, largely undisclosed pattern of military responses in the Gulf region following the joint US-Israeli strikes on Iran.

events.title: US Senate Blocks Latest Bid to Limit Trump's Iran War Powers
events.desc: US Senate Republicans have again blocked a Democratic-led resolution aimed at ending the war in Iran until Congress authorizes it. While the measure failed to advance, it garnered increased support, with three Republicans joining Democrats. This vote highlights growing divisions within the Republican party regarding the ongoing conflict.

events.title: US Seeks China's Help to Persuade Iran to Change Course
events.desc: The United States is hoping to enlist China's assistance in convincing Iran to de-escalate its actions in the Gulf. Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated that while China might encourage Iran to return to negotiations, Beijing is unlikely to abandon its economic support for its key Middle Eastern partner or cease supplying dual-use goods.

events.title: Lebanon Reports 13 Killed in Israeli Strikes, Including Two Paramedics
events.desc: Lebanon's health ministry reported that 13 individuals, including two paramedics, were killed in Israeli strikes in Nabatieh. The paramedics were reportedly responding to an earlier attack that had claimed one life. The strikes occurred despite a recent ceasefire announcement, indicating continued exchanges of fire between Israel and Hezbollah.

events.title: Lebanon Claims 39 Dead in Israeli Strikes Amidst Ceasefire
events.desc: Lebanon has stated that 39 people were killed in recent Israeli strikes, underscoring the continued conflict between Israel and Hezbollah despite a ceasefire agreement announced last month. This escalation highlights the fragility of the current truce and ongoing hostilities in the region.

events.title: Iranian Police Infrastructure Targeted in War Efforts
events.desc: Bellingcat has documented damage or destruction to at least 80 police stations and infrastructure linked to law enforcement and the Basij paramilitary group in the first three weeks of the US-Israeli war against Iran. Experts suggest this is part of a strategy to degrade the Iranian regime's 'repressive capacity'.

events.title: Iran Demands Guarantees for World Cup Participation
events.desc: The Iranian Football Federation (FFIRI) has presented FIFA with 10 conditions to ensure its participation in the 2026 World Cup, co-hosted by the United States, Mexico, and Canada. This development comes amidst broader tensions and ongoing conflicts in the region.

events.title: Netanyahu Made Secret Visit to UAE During Iran War
events.desc: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu reportedly made a secret visit to the United Arab Emirates during the war with Iran, meeting with Emirati President Mohammed bin Zayed. The Prime Minister's Office described the meeting as a 'historic breakthrough,' though the UAE initially denied reports of an 'alleged visit.'

events.title: US-Israeli Strikes on Iran Expose Limitations of Asian Partnerships in Hormuz Crisis
events.desc: The ongoing crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, triggered by US-Israeli strikes on Iran, highlights a paradox: the closure of the vital waterway is primarily an Asian problem, yet Asian nations are largely absent from resolution efforts. South Korea's hesitant response to a US request for naval support underscores this disconnect, even as Asian economies suffer significant impacts.

events.title: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Nearly 800 Hungarian government email addresses and associated passwords have been leaked online, revealing significant security vulnerabilities. Bellingcat's analysis indicates that 12 out of 13 ministries were affected, potentially exposing sensitive information. The passwords included trivial terms like 'Snoopy', 'Adolf', and 'Password'.

events.title: Iran's Police Infrastructure Targeted Amidst War
events.desc: In the initial three weeks of the United States and Israel's war against Iran, at least 80 police stations and facilities associated with law enforcement and the Basij paramilitary group have been damaged or destroyed. Experts suggest this campaign aims to degrade the Iranian regime's capacity for repression.

events.title: Pakistan's Logic of Limited War Risks Escalation
events.desc: Pakistan's decision in May 2025 to refrain from using its Babur cruise missile against Indian BrahMos strikes, fearing nuclear escalation, exemplifies a new logic of limited war. However, analysts warn that this strategy may not inherently keep conflicts contained, as demonstrated by the sustained air campaign following the four-day crisis.

events.title: US and China Leaders Discuss Trade, Tech, and Iran
events.desc: President Trump and President Xi are holding summit talks covering trade, technology, and the ongoing war with Iran. This meeting occurs at a critical juncture for the global economy, with the Iran conflict significantly disrupting international trade.

events.title: Iran Slams 'Collusion' Amidst Netanyahu's Secret UAE Visit During War
events.desc: Iran has criticized what it calls 'collusion' following reports of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's secret visit to the United Arab Emirates during the war with Iran. Iran's first vice president asserted Tehran's 'established' right to the Strait of Hormuz, declaring the matter closed.
